stemHtml: According to OPS regulations, an operator shall not operate a turbine propeller powered aeroplane with a maximum certificated take-off mass in excess of 5700 kg or having a maximum approved passenger seating configuration of more than nine seats or a turbojet powered aeroplane unless it is equipped with an altitude alerting system capable of alerting the flight crew when…

options.a: Exceeding the service ceiling of the aeroplane.
options.b: Cabin altitude exceeds 10 000 ft (cabin pressure below 700 hPa).
options.d: Flying over terrain that exceeds the maximum one-engine-inoperative cruising altitude.
